title: Topological Metal-Insulator Transition within the Ferromagnetic state

paper_analysis.summary: K2Cr8O16 is shown to undergo a ferromagnetic metal-insulator transition (FM-MIT) in which the insulating state emerges without loss of ferromagnetic order. Combining neutron scattering, inelastic x-ray scattering, magnetization, and first-principles calculations, the authors argue that the transition is not Peierls-driven (no phonon softening) but instead corresponds to a correlation-assisted topological transition where Weyl points present in the metallic ferromagnetic phase disappear in the monoclinic insulating phase.
measurement_types: density functional perturbation theory / phonon calculations
Wannier-based Berry curvature / Weyl point analysis

figures.description: Crystal structures of K2Cr8O16 in the tetragonal metallic phase and monoclinic insulating phase, showing the chimney/double-chain motif and lattice vectors.
Magnetic Cr sublattice model with exchange paths J1, J2, J3 and the inferred ferromagnetic spin arrangement.
Angle-dependent magnetization at 120 K for several applied fields, fitted with a sinusoidal function to determine the in-plane easy-axis orientation.
Neutron powder diffraction pattern at 10 K with Rietveld refinement and magnetic reflection assignment; inset shows single-crystal magnetic refinement comparison.
Temperature dependence of neutron diffraction intensities at (2,1,1) and (1,2,1) reflections across the MIT.
Powder INS spectra at 5 K collected with Ei = 9 meV.
Powder INS spectra at 130 K collected with Ei = 9 meV.
Spin-wave/Heisenberg-model fit to the 5 K INS spectra, extracting exchange parameters.
Spin-wave/Heisenberg-model fit to the 130 K INS spectra, showing similar exchange couplings across the MIT.
Local CrO6 octahedron schematic showing compression and local orbital axes.
Calculated Weyl points in the tetragonal Brillouin zone and the nesting vector qnest connecting opposite-chirality nodes.
DFT band structure of the tetragonal metallic phase (U = 0 eV) with t2g orbital character projection and Weyl-point location indicated.
DFT band structure of the monoclinic insulating phase (U = 4 eV) with orbital projections, showing gap opening and orbital splitting.
IXS energy scans at q near qCDW for 20 K, 95 K, and 115 K, showing phonon peaks and elastic scattering.
Integrated elastic scattering intensity across qCDW as a function of temperature, fitted to a mean-field order-parameter form.
Transverse phonon dispersion across qCDW at multiple temperatures, showing no phonon softening.
Longitudinal phonon dispersion across qCDW at multiple temperatures, also showing no phonon condensation.
Comparison of measured phonon dispersion from Γ to M with calculated phonon dispersion from first principles.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include neutron powder diffraction patterns, single-crystal neutron diffraction peak intensities, inelastic neutron scattering spectra at multiple incident energies and temperatures, inelastic x-ray scattering energy scans and phonon dispersions, and angle-dependent magnetization curves. The paper also reports first-principles electronic-structure, exchange, Berry-curvature, and phonon calculations.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states: 'All data needed to evaluate the conclusions of this study are available within the Article and its Supplementary Information. All datasets generated and/or analyzed during the current study are available from the corresponding authors upon request.' No public repository or downloadable archive is mentioned in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw experimental data were collected on specific instruments: IXS at SPring-8 BL35XU (Ei = 21.74 keV), NPD at SNS/POWGEN, single-crystal ND at PSI/ZEBRA, INS at J-PARC/4SEASON with multiple incident energies, and magnetization on a Quantum Design MPMS3. The paper does not specify file formats or a public raw-data archive.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include Rietveld refinement profiles, extracted peak intensities versus temperature, fitted INS spectra and exchange parameters (J1, J2, J3), fitted magnetization angular dependence, fitted elastic intensity order-parameter curves, and derived phonon dispersions. The paper also reports DFT-derived band structures, Wannier downfolding results, Berry curvature/Weyl point locations, and DFPT phonon dispersions.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-ready data are clearly present in the article and Supplementary Information as plotted diffraction patterns, spectra, dispersions, and fitted curves. The text does not explicitly mention a separate source-data repository or figure-source-data files.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: Analysis software/packages are named (FullProf, SpinW, Dave, Quantum ESPRESSO, Phonopy, Wannier90, TB2J, WannierTools), but no custom analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repository are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is a mixed experimental-theoretical package rather than a single tabular dataset. Experimental data are modality-specific and instrument-specific, while theoretical data are derived from DFT/DFPT and Wannier-based post-processing. The paper emphasizes that supplementary material contains additional analysis details and figures, but the provided text does not indicate open-source raw files or code. The most important semantic distinction is that many published results are processed/fitted outputs from raw scattering or magnetization measurements, not raw detector images or event lists.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Methods section explicitly describes multiple measurements on raw experimental instruments: neutron powder diffraction, single-crystal neutron diffraction, INS, IXS, and magnetization measurements.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper presents fitted Rietveld profiles, fitted spectra, extracted exchange constants, phonon dispersions, and DFT/Wannier-derived band/topology results, which are figure-ready processed data.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: All data needed to evaluate the conclusions of this study are available within the Article and its Supplementary Information. All datasets generated and/or analyzed during the current study are available from the corresponding authors upon request.
